accessLogging.^description: You have not enabled CloudFront standard logs (access logs) for your distribution(s). CloudFront standard logs provide detailed records about every request that's made to a distribution. These logs are useful for many scenarios, including security and access audits.

WAFAssociation.^description: You have not associated either WAF or WAFv2 web ACL with your Amazon CloudFront distributions. AWS WAF helps you protect against common web exploits and bots that can affect availability, compromise security, or consume excessive resources.
WAFAssociation.shortDesc: Use Web Application Firewall (WAF) for enhanced security.

defaultRootObject.^description: You have not configured CloudFront to return a specific object (the default root object) when a user requests the root URL for your distribution instead of requesting an object in your distribution. Specifying a default root object lets you avoid exposing the contents of your distribution.
defaultRootObject.shortDesc: Specify a default root object for your distribution.

compressObjectsAutomatically.^description: You have not configured CloudFront to automatically compress certain types of objects (files) and serve the compressed objects when viewers (web browsers or other clients) support them. When requested objects are compressed, downloads can be faster because the objects are smaller.
compressObjectsAutomatically.shortDesc: Configure CloudFront to compress objects automatically

DeprecatedSSLProtocol.^description: Your CloudFront distributions are using deprecated SSL protocols for HTTPS communication between CloudFront edge locations and custom origins.
DeprecatedSSLProtocol.shortDesc: Avoid using SSLv3 in your CloudFront distributions.

originFailover.^description: Your CloudFront distributions is not setup for origin failover. To set up origin failover, you must have a distribution with at least two origins.
originFailover.shortDesc: Create an origin group with two origins: a primary and a secondary.

fieldLevelEncryption.^description: Field-level encryption adds an additional layer of security that lets you protect specific data throughout system processing so that only certain applications can see it.
fieldLevelEncryption.shortDesc: Set-up field-level encryption for your CloudFront distributions.

viewerPolicyHttps.^description: Your CloudFront distribution does not enforce HTTPS for communication between viewers and CloudFront.
viewerPolicyHttps.shortDesc: Configure one or more cache behaviors in your CloudFront distribution to require HTTPS for communication between viewers and CloudFront.

S3OriginAccessControl.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) have S3 origins without Origin Access Control (OAC) or Origin Access Identity (OAI), allowing direct public access to S3 buckets
S3OriginAccessControl.shortDesc: Configure Origin Access Control (OAC) for S3 origins to restrict direct access

OriginTrafficEncryption.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) have custom origins that do not enforce HTTPS for communication between CloudFront and the origin
OriginTrafficEncryption.shortDesc: Require HTTPS for communication between CloudFront and custom origins
ref: [Require HTTPS for communication between CloudFront and your custom origin]<https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/using-https-cloudfront-to-custom-origin.html>

CustomSSLCertificate.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) are using the default CloudFront SSL certificate (*.cloudfront.net) instead of a custom certificate
CustomSSLCertificate.shortDesc: Use custom SSL/TLS certificates for production distributions

SNIConfiguration.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) with custom SSL certificates are using dedicated IP addresses instead of SNI, incurring $600/month per distribution
SNIConfiguration.shortDesc: Use SNI (Server Name Indication) instead of dedicated IPs for custom SSL certificates

S3OriginBucketExists.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) have S3 origins pointing to non-existent or inaccessible S3 buckets
S3OriginBucketExists.shortDesc: Ensure S3 origin buckets exist and are accessible

OriginShieldEnabled.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) do not have Origin Shield enabled, missing opportunity to reduce origin load and improve cache hit ratio
OriginShieldEnabled.shortDesc: Consider enabling Origin Shield to reduce origin load

GeoRestrictionsConfigured.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) do not have geographic restrictions configured, allowing access from all countries
GeoRestrictionsConfigured.shortDesc: Consider configuring geographic restrictions for compliance and security

PriceClassOptimization.^description: {$COUNT} CloudFront distribution(s) are using all edge locations (PriceClass_All), which may be more expensive than necessary
PriceClassOptimization.shortDesc: Review price class to optimize costs based on geographic requirements
ref: [Choosing the price class for a CloudFront distribution]<https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/PriceClass.html>
